prompt:
You are an autonomous research-and-coding agent working in a local llama.cpp + Gemma repo.
        Your job is to read the evidence packet, reason about the engineering tradeoff, and answer the task.

        KV-CACHE QUANTIZATION RESEARCH PACKET
- [kivi] KIVI: A Tuning-Free Asymmetric 2bit Quantization for KV Cache: KV cache memory grows with batch size and context length and can become the serving bottleneck. KIVI argues key cache and value cache have different quantization structure: K per-channel, V per-token. KV-cache quantization should be tested with quality, peak memory, and throughput, not memory alone.
- [hf_transformers_kv_cache] Transformers cache strategies: QuantizedCache: Hugging Face exposes a QuantizedCache to reduce KV memory. The documented backends include HQQ with int2, int4, int8 and Quanto with int2, int4. The cache implementation is an inference-time strategy, distinct from model-weight quantization.
- [hf_blog_kv_cache_quant] Unlocking Longer Generation with Key-Value Cache Quantization: KV-cache quantization is useful when longer context or larger batch would otherwise hit memory limits. Evaluation should include long-generation and long-context behavior, not only short prompt quality. Quantized KV implementations can keep a small residual cache in higher precision before quantizing.
- [turboquant] TurboQuant: Online Vector Quantization with Near-optimal Distortion Rate: TurboQuant targets online vector quantization, including KV-cache quantization. The paper optimizes both mean-squared error and inner-product distortion. The reported KV-cache setting highlights very low bit budgets, so evals must check attention-sensitive tasks.
- [llama_cpp_cache_types] llama.cpp CLI/server cache type flags: llama.cpp exposes separate --cache-type-k and --cache-type-v flags. The local build documents f32, f16, bf16, q8_0, q4_0, q4_1, iq4_nl, q5_0, q5_1. The /completion endpoint returns timings and token counts that can be used for eval summaries.

LOCAL RUNTIME SURFACE
- llama.cpp server supports separate --cache-type-k and --cache-type-v flags.
- Candidate cache types in this build: f32, f16, bf16, q8_0, q5_0, q5_1, q4_0, q4_1, iq4_nl.
- Existing Gemma calibration uses flash attention and a balanced q8_0/q5_1 KV setting.
- A useful first matrix compares f16/f16 baseline, q8_0/q8_0, q8_0/q5_1, q5_1/q5_1, q4_0/q4_0, and iq4_nl/iq4_nl.

        LONG WORKSPACE CONTEXT
        Context block 00:
Repository note: the dashboard renders a static MoE expert map and separate routing traces. Repeat marker 00.
Operational note: generated artifacts should live under data/kv_cache_eval/runs and should not be committed by default. Repeat marker 00.
Measurement note: prompt processing throughput and decode throughput can move in different directions. Repeat marker 00.
Quality note: long-context retrieval failures can look like confident implementation mistakes. Repeat marker 00.
Implementation note: keep cache_prompt disabled when comparing full prompt processing across cache formats. Repeat marker 00.
Agent note: a good answer should state assumptions, propose a sweep, and define pass/fail criteria. Repeat marker 00.

        TASK

            Write a research memo that explains why KV-cache quantization is worth testing for long-context
            agentic workloads. Include the expected tradeoff, the difference between model-weight quantization
            and KV-cache quantization, and the specific llama.cpp cache types we should sweep first.


        RESPONSE FORMAT
        Use concise markdown with these headings:
        - Decision
        - Evidence
        - Implementation
        - Validation
        Preserve exact flag and cache-type spellings when they matter, such as --cache-type-k, --cache-type-v,
        f16, q8_0, q5_1, q4_0, and iq4_nl.
title: Research memo for a KV-cache quant sweep

The local build documents f32, f16, bf16, q8_0, q4_0, q4_1, iq4_nl, q5_0, q5_1. The /completion endpoint returns timings and token counts that can be used for eval summaries.

LOCAL RUNTIME SURFACE
- llama.cpp server supports separate --cache-type-k and --cache-type-v flags.
- Candidate cache types in this build: f32, f16, bf16, q8_0, q5_0, q5_1, q4_0, q4_1, iq4_nl.
- Existing Gemma calibration uses flash attention and a balanced q8_0/q5_1 KV setting.
- A useful first matrix compares f16/f16 baseline, q8_0/q8_0, q8_0/q5_1, q5_1/q5_1, q4_0/q4_0, and iq4_nl/iq4_nl.
